DISCOVERY, 302, HEARING ON DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MALAK'S DEPOSITION; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS HELD. DEFENDANT STIPULATES TO THE PRO TEM JUDGE. THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT TIMELY CONTESTED. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT: WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak?S Deposition; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak'S Deposition; Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom):
ANASTASIA S. MCMURRY, Esq., of VOGL MEREDITH BURKE & STREZA LLP, (415) 398-0200, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "SAFEWAY") MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MALAK TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION WITHIN THE NEXT THIRTY (30) CALENDAR DAYS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS UNOPPOSED. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN THIRTY (30) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION VIA ZOOM AT A DATE AND TIME CHOSEN BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WITH THE SPECIFIED DATE INCLUDED IN THE PROPOSED ORDER,.
(2) DEFENDANT SHALL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF AT HIS LAST KNOWN ADDRESS AN AMENDED NOTICE OF DEPOSITION FOR THE DATE CHOSEN BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION INCLUDING THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. THE SPECIFIED DOCUMENTS MUST BE PRODUCED BY PLAINTIFF EITHER BEFORE OR AT THE DEPOSITION.
(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF TWO THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED AND SIXTY DOLLARS ($2,460.00) PAYABLE WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR SAFEWAY SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON JANUARY 23, 2024 REGARDLESS AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS SIGNED AGREEMENT AS TO FORM.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom):
ANASTASIA S. MCMURRY, Esq., of VOGL MEREDITH BURKE & STREZA LLP, (415) 398-0200, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "SAFEWAY") MOTION TO REQUEST THAT SAFEWAY'S THIRD SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION TO PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MALAK (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR"MALAK") BE DEEMED ADMITTED IS UNOPPOSED. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER TO DEFENDANT WITHOUT OBJECTOION VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION.
(2) TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY RESPONSE IS OTHER THAN AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER TO DEFENDANT WITHOUT OBJECTION VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES NO. 17.1
(3) IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF PARAGRAPHS (1 & (2) ABOVE WITHIN THE TIME SPECIFIED, ALL REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFF WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE ADMISSION.
(4) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS ($1,000.00) PAYABLE TO SAFEWAY AND ITS ATTORNEYS WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak'S Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak'S Independent Medical Examination.

Appearances (via Zoom):
ANASTASIA S. MCMURRY, Esq., of VOGL MEREDITH BURKE & STREZA LLP, (415) 398-0200, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "SAFEWAY") MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MALAK (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "MALAK") TO SUMIT TO AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINNATION (HEREAFTER "IME") WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS UNOPPOSED. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN FIVE CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, COUNSEL FOR SAFEWAY WILL SEND TO PLAINTIFF'S LAST KNOWN ADDRESS A DEMAND FOR AN IME IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROVISIONS OF CCP SECTION 2032.220,ET SEQ. FOR A DATE CHOSEN BY DEFENDANT NOT LESS THAN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.
(2) PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH DEFENDANT'S DEMAND FOR AN IME AT THE DATE AND TIME SPECIFIED IN THE DEMAND, UNLESS WITHIN FIVE (5) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE THAT DEFENDANT SENT ITS DEMAND PLAINTIFF HAS ADVISED DEFENDANT THAT HE OBJECTS TO THE DEMAND AND THE GROUNDS OF HIS OBJECTION(S).
(3) THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION TO ENABLE IT TO RESOLVE PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION(S). EITHER PLAINTIFF OR SAFEWAY SHALL PROMPTLY BRING TO THE COURT'S ATTENTION THE OBJECTION(S) TO ENABLE A RESOLUTION PRIOR TO THE DATE DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL HAS SET FOR THE IME.
(4) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AS TO THIS MOTION.
COUNSEL FOR SAFEWAY SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON JANUARY 23, 2024 REGARDLESS AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS SIGNED AGREEMENT AS TO FORM.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak'S Deposition; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-19-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Anastasia S. McMurry, Esq. of Vogl Meredith Burke & Streza LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, for Defendant Safeway, Inc.;
No other appearance by any party or counsel.

Hearing on DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Malak's Deposition; Request For Sanctions.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for Defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Brief discussions are held. Ms. McMurry informs the Court that she did not timely contest the tentative ruling.

The tentative ruling was not timely contested. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Motion denied without prejudice. The Court first notes that moving party did not comply with local rule re courtesy copy of moving papers. The Court is concerned about the lack of due process as the motion was served upon Plaintiff's former counsel on the very same day Plaintiff's former counsel was substituted by Plaintiff in pro per.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at: williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Matter not reported. (JPT/302)
